phishing.title: How to Identify a Safe Domain
phishing.body:
Every website on the internet lives at an address called a domain name. Knowing how to read that address is the single most effective skill for avoiding phishing scams. Most fake Roblox sites are designed to look identical to the real one — the only giveaway is the domain in your browser's address bar.

The Golden Rule

The only safe domain for Roblox is roblox.com — specifically on the .com top-level domain with nothing extra added before or after. Any variation must be treated as suspicious until proven otherwise.

Safe vs. Suspicious: Real Examples

Here are examples of what safe and suspicious domains look like. Train your eyes to spot the difference immediately.

roblox.comSafe
www.roblox.comSafe
roblox.com.freerobux.xyzDangerous
roblox-support.comDangerous
roblox.netDangerous
roblox.ioDangerous
getrobux.roblox.siteDangerous
robIox.comDangerous — note the capital 'I' instead of lowercase 'l'

The Two Attack Patterns

Pattern 1: Extra text appended after the real domain

This is the most common trick. The attacker registers a domain that begins with a familiar name but adds words after the .com:

roblox.com.login-verify.netDangerous

Your browser reads domains right to left at the dot boundaries. The real domain here is login-verify.net — not roblox.com. The roblox.com part is just a subdomain label used to deceive you.

Pattern 2: Different top-level domain (TLD) replacing .com

An attacker registers the same brand name but with a different suffix like .net, .io, .xyz, .site, .gg, etc.:

roblox.netDangerous
roblox.ggDangerous

Roblox does not operate on any of these. If the suffix isn't .com, treat it as suspicious.

How to Check the Address Bar

  • Always look at the address bar before entering any credentials.
  • Click on the address bar to see the full URL — browsers sometimes hide parts of it.
  • Look for the padlock (HTTPS) — but be aware that fake sites can also use HTTPS. The lock does not mean the site is legitimate; it only means the connection is encrypted.
  • If you're ever unsure, close the tab and navigate to roblox.com manually by typing it yourself.
⚠️
Free Robux Sites
Any site that claims to give you free Robux in exchange for your username and password is a phishing site — no exceptions. Roblox does not have any official partner program that grants free Robux through third-party sites.

phishing.title: How Leaked Credentials Get Exploited
phishing.body:
Data breaches happen regularly across the internet. When a platform you use gets hacked, your email address and password can end up on public leak lists that anyone can download. Even if Roblox itself is never breached, your account is still at risk if you reuse passwords from other sites.

What Attackers Do With Leaked Data

Credential Stuffing

This is the most common automated attack. A attacker downloads a leaked database — which might contain millions of email/password pairs — and runs a script that automatically tries each combination on Roblox's login page. They don't need to guess anything; they're using your real credentials.

If you used the same password on Roblox as on another site that got breached, your account can be compromised within minutes of the breach becoming public — even if you never clicked a phishing link.

Session Token Theft

More advanced attackers steal your browser's session cookies using malware or malicious scripts. With a valid session token, they can access your account without ever knowing your password — bypassing even basic password security. This is why logging out of inactive sessions matters.

Bypassing Account Security Quickly

Once inside, attackers act fast — usually within seconds using automated tools. They will:

  • Change your account email to one they control
  • Change your password immediately
  • Remove or replace 2FA settings
  • Spend or transfer your Robux and limited items
  • Remove your recovery methods so you can't get back in
🚨
Speed of Attack
Account takeovers are often fully automated. From the moment an attacker logs in, they can lock you out and drain your account in under 60 seconds using scripts. This is why prevention is the only reliable strategy — recovery is difficult and often incomplete.

How to Protect Yourself

Use a unique password for every account

This is the single most impactful thing you can do. If your Roblox password is unique, a breach on any other platform cannot affect it. Use a password manager like Bitwarden (free, open-source) or your browser's built-in password manager to generate and store strong, unique passwords.

Check if your credentials are leaked

Visit haveibeenpwned.com and enter your email address. If it appears in any known breach, change that password immediately on every site where you used it.

Enable 2FA immediately

Even if an attacker has your password, 2FA requires a second code from your device that they don't have. See the Account Security section for setup instructions.

💡
Pro Tip
Use your email address's plus alias feature when signing up for services. For example, if your email is you@gmail.com, sign up as you+roblox@gmail.com. You'll still receive mail, but if that address appears in a breach, you know exactly which service was compromised.

phishing.title: Link Your Email Early for Recovery
phishing.body:
One of the most underrated security steps on Roblox is something you can do right now in under two minutes: link a Gmail or email address to your account. This single action dramatically improves your ability to recover your account if something goes wrong.

Why It Matters So Much

When attackers take over an account, their first move is always to lock you out. They change the password, the email, and remove 2FA. Without a linked and verified email already on the account, Roblox has very limited ways to confirm that you're the real owner.

A verified email address gives you:

  • Password reset access — you can recover the account without contacting support
  • Account verification — Roblox can send a confirmation link to prove ownership
  • Security alerts — you'll receive notifications if login attempts happen from new devices
  • Faster support escalation — if you do need help from Roblox, having a verified email on file speeds up identity verification

How to Link Your Email

  1. Log in to Roblox and click the menu icon in the top-left corner.
  2. Go to Settings — the gear icon or from the dropdown menu.
  3. Under Account Info, find the Email section and enter your Gmail or email address.
  4. Verify the email by clicking the confirmation link sent to your inbox. This step is critical — an unverified email provides no protection.
📧
Gmail Recommended
Gmail is recommended because it has strong account security of its own (2FA via Google, passkey support, recovery options). A secure email account is the master key to your Roblox account — so protect your email as carefully as Roblox itself.

Secure Your Email Too

Your email account is the recovery method for everything else. If an attacker takes over your email, they can reset passwords for every account linked to it — including Roblox. Make sure your Gmail has:

  • A strong, unique password
  • 2-Step Verification enabled (Google Authenticator or passkey)
  • Recovery phone number or backup email configured
⚠️
Do This Before You Get Hacked
It's far easier to link an email when you're in control of your account than to try to prove ownership after a takeover. Don't wait for an incident — take 2 minutes to do this today.

phishing.title: Using Roblox Customer Support If Compromised
phishing.body:
If your account has already been compromised and you've been locked out, your path forward is Roblox's official Customer Support. It's important to go directly to the correct channel — scammers often impersonate Roblox support on Discord, YouTube comments, and social media.

Official Support Channel

The only official place to get help from Roblox is:

en.help.roblox.com/hc/en-us/requests/newOfficial

Go there yourself by typing it in the address bar. Do not click links to "Roblox support" from Discord messages, YouTube comments, or other players — these are almost always scams designed to steal additional information from you.

What to Prepare Before Submitting a Ticket

Roblox Support will need to verify that you're the original owner. The more evidence you can provide, the faster your case will be resolved. Gather:

  • Original username — the exact username of the compromised account
  • Email address — the email that was originally linked to the account, if any
  • Account creation date — approximate year/month
  • Original IP address or location — where you typically played from
  • Purchase history — any Robux transactions, gift card codes you've used, or Premium subscription details
  • Original password — even if it's been changed, knowing the original can help verify ownership
📋
Be Patient
Account recovery through support can take several days. Submit your ticket with as much detail as possible, and respond promptly to any follow-up emails from Roblox. Check your spam folder for their replies.

Warning: Fake Support Scams

After losing an account, many players search for help in communities and are targeted by fake "support agents" offering to recover the account — often for a fee or in exchange for personal details. This is always a scam. Signs of a fake support agent:

  • Reaching out to you first (Roblox support does not DM you)
  • Asking for your password (legitimate support never needs this)
  • Asking for payment to recover your account
  • Claiming to be a "Roblox employee" on a third-party platform
  • Asking you to visit a link to "verify" your account
🚫
Never Pay For Recovery
No legitimate service charges money to recover a Roblox account. Anyone asking for payment, gift cards, cryptocurrency, or personal information in exchange for account recovery is scamming you. Do not engage — block and report them.

security.title: Building a Secure Account Foundation
security.body:
The most effective security measures are ones you set up before anything bad happens. Once an attacker is inside your account, they act in seconds — locking you out before you even realize there's a problem. This guide covers everything you should configure right now while you're still in control.

1. Link a Verified Email Address

Your email is the master key for account recovery. Without it, proving ownership to Roblox Support becomes much harder.

  • Go to Settings → Account Info and add your Gmail or email.
  • Click the verification link in your inbox — an unverified email provides no protection.
  • Make sure the email account itself is secured with its own 2FA and a strong unique password.

2. Add a Phone Number

A verified phone number provides an additional recovery pathway and enables Roblox to send you security alerts via SMS. It also adds an extra layer of identity verification if you ever need to contact Support.

  • Go to Settings → Account Info → Phone.
  • Enter and verify your phone number with the SMS code sent to you.
📱
Layered Defense
Think of email and phone as two independent escape hatches. If an attacker removes one, having the other still on file significantly increases your recovery options. Enable both.

3. Enable Two-Step Verification (2FA)

2FA adds a second checkpoint after your password. Even if an attacker knows your password, they still cannot log in without the one-time code from your device. See the next article for detailed setup instructions for both Google Authenticator and Passkeys.

4. Set Up Parental Controls (Even If You're Not a Minor)

Roblox's parental controls include account restrictions that can prevent unauthorized changes, restrict chat, and limit who can interact with your account. Even for adult players, enabling some of these settings reduces your attack surface.

  • Go to Settings → Parental Controls (may appear as Privacy in some regions).
  • Set a separate Account PIN — this PIN is required to change sensitive settings, adding a layer of protection even if an attacker gets your password and is already logged in.
  • Review who can message and follow your account.
🔑
Account PIN
The Account PIN in parental controls is separate from your password. If an attacker logs in and tries to change your email or security settings, they'll be blocked by this PIN. It's an underused but highly effective safeguard — enable it with a PIN you don't use anywhere else.

Security Checklist

  • ✅ Verified email address linked
  • ✅ Phone number verified
  • ✅ 2FA enabled (Authenticator App or Passkey)
  • ✅ Recovery codes saved in a secure location
  • ✅ Account PIN set in Parental Controls
  • ✅ Unique, strong password not used on any other site
  • ✅ Email account also secured with 2FA

security.title: Setting Up 2FA & Recovery Codes
security.body:
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is the single most impactful security upgrade you can make to your Roblox account. Even if your password is stolen or leaked, an attacker cannot access your account without the second factor — which only you have on your device.

Method 1: Google Authenticator (Recommended)

An authenticator app generates a time-based one-time code (TOTP) that changes every 30 seconds. It works offline and doesn't rely on SMS, which can be intercepted via SIM-swap attacks.

  1. Install an authenticator app on your phone. Google Authenticator (iOS/Android) or Aegis (Android, open-source) are good choices.
  2. Log in to Roblox and go to Settings → Security.
  3. Enable 2-Step Verification and select Authenticator App.
  4. Scan the QR code shown on screen with your authenticator app. If you can't scan it, use the manual code provided instead.
  5. Enter the 6-digit code from your app to confirm setup. Roblox will verify it's working before enabling.
  6. Save your recovery codes — shown in the next step. This is critical. See below.
⚠️
If You Lose Your Phone
If you lose your phone without recovery codes saved, you can be permanently locked out of your own account — even if you know the password and email. Recovery codes are your insurance policy. Always save them.

Method 2: Passkey

A Passkey is a modern, phishing-resistant authentication method that uses your device's biometrics (fingerprint, Face ID, or PIN) instead of a code. It's tied to the specific website, so it cannot be used on a fake phishing site — making it more secure than a password or OTP code.

  1. Go to Settings → Security on Roblox.
  2. Select Passkey under 2-Step Verification or sign-in methods (availability may vary by device).
  3. Follow your device's prompts — your device will register a passkey using Face ID, fingerprint, or device PIN.
  4. Confirm the setup by completing a biometric or PIN challenge.
💡
Passkey + Authenticator Together
You can set up both a Passkey and an Authenticator App as backup methods. Using both gives you the convenience of biometrics on your main device and the ability to log in with a code on a different device if needed.

Recovery Codes — Your Most Important Backup

When you enable 2FA, Roblox generates 10 single-use recovery codes. These codes let you log in if you lose access to your 2FA device. Each code can only be used once — after use, it's permanently invalid.

How to save your recovery codes safely

  • Print them out and keep the paper in a secure physical location (not next to your computer).
  • Save to a password manager — store them as a secure note in Bitwarden, 1Password, or similar.
  • Screenshot and store offline — save to a USB drive or encrypted storage, not just cloud photos.
  • Write them down — old-fashioned but effective. Store in a safe or locked drawer.
🚨
Never Share Recovery Codes
Recovery codes bypass 2FA entirely. Anyone who has a recovery code can log into your account without your password or authenticator app. Treat them like a master key — never share them with anyone, including people claiming to be from Roblox support.

Regenerating codes

If you think your recovery codes have been compromised, or you've used most of them, go to Settings → Security → 2-Step Verification and generate a new set. The old codes immediately become invalid when you generate new ones.
